data.narrative: The student transformed an ambiguous feature request into a structured implementation plan using progressive decomposition, explicit interface contracts, and verification-driven prompt refinement. They correctly identified an architectural conflict between dependent tasks, resolved it by defining a shared contract, and improved downstream prompt consistency.

dimension_scores.observation: Created structured prompts with explicit scope, dependencies, and acceptance criteria.
dimension_scores.evidence: Prepared dependency-ordered implementation prompts and refined them after verification.

dimension_scores.observation: Compared generated output against acceptance criteria and identified hidden assumptions.
dimension_scores.evidence: Detected the mismatch between owner_id and team_members data models.

dimension_scores.observation: Resolved integration issues by updating prompts instead of restarting the decomposition.
dimension_scores.evidence: Introduced explicit interface contracts and re-executed the corrected prompt.

dimension_scores.observation: Made an architectural decision instead of accepting conflicting AI assumptions.
dimension_scores.evidence: Defined a producer-consumer interface contract for shared database structures.

dimension_scores.observation: Produced a reviewable implementation plan with verification checkpoints.
dimension_scores.evidence: Verified prompt output against predefined acceptance criteria before accepting it.

strengths: Systematically decomposed an ambiguous feature into dependency-aware implementation tasks.
Recognized and resolved architectural inconsistencies using explicit interface contracts.
data.recommendation: Continue improving prompt context by explicitly referencing verified upstream artifacts before generating downstream implementations.

public_evidence.student_action: Designed a dependency-driven task tree with interface contracts and acceptance criteria.
public_evidence.technical_detail: Prompt decomposition, interface contracts, dependency ordering, acceptance criteria.
public_evidence.proof: Completed Module 2 BUILD, VERIFY, BREAK and FIX activities with verified prompt refinement.
public_evidence.ability: Prompt decomposition and AI-assisted engineering planning.
public_evidence.reviewer_value: Demonstrates the ability to coordinate multi-step AI implementation workflows while maintaining architectural consistency.

concepts_demonstrated.evidence: Resolved integration conflict by defining explicit producer-consumer contracts.
Reviewed AI output against acceptance criteria before acceptance.

teaching_approaches.detail: Used concrete implementation examples and dependency analysis.

learning_style_signals: Responds well to concrete engineering examples and artifact-based verification.
